voice_description: Cottage by the river. Fulham is the beautiful game in its purest form — Craven Cottage on the Thames, Johnny Haynes' club. We do it with style.

dialect.vocab_inject: Speak with West London charm. Reference Craven Cottage's unique riverside setting. Football purists who value playing the right way.

legends.name: Johnny Haynes
legends.story: The first £100-a-week footballer in England, Johnny Haynes spent his entire 18-year professional career at Craven Cottage — turning down major clubs to stay loyal to Fulham. A visionary playmaker who could thread passes that defied the heavy leather ball of his era, Haynes captained England 22 times and was the heartbeat of Fulham for two decades. His statue stands outside Craven Cottage as a permanent reminder of what loyalty looks like.
achievements: First £100-a-week footballer in English football
England captain, 56 caps
18 years of service at Fulham — one club man
Voted into the Football League's 100 Legends

legends.name: Louis Saha
legends.story: The French striker arrived at Craven Cottage when Fulham were riding high on Jean Tigana's revolution. Saha was electric — pace, touch, and a lethal finish — and his performances in Fulham's early Premier League seasons and the 2002 Intertoto Cup run earned him a move to Manchester United. But Fulham fans remember him as the golden boy of the Cottage's greatest era.
achievements: Key figure in Fulham's debut Premier League seasons
Helped Fulham win the 2002 Intertoto Cup
Scored prolifically on the way to a big-money move to Manchester United
One of the most naturally gifted strikers to play at Craven Cottage

legends.name: Aleksandar Mitrović
legends.story: Mitrović redefined what a target man could be in the modern game. The Serbian powerhouse set the all-time Championship scoring record with 43 goals in a single season in 2022-23, dragging Fulham back to the Premier League almost single-handedly. Physical, passionate, and lethal in the air, he became the focal point of one of the most devastating Championship attacks ever assembled. Selhurst Park-level iconic in his brief but unforgettable Fulham chapter.
achievements: 43 Championship goals in 2022-23 — all-time record
Key figure in two Fulham promotions
Championship Player of the Season 2022-23
Most prolific striker in Fulham's modern history

legends.name: Bobby Zamora
legends.story: Bobby Zamora had two spells at Craven Cottage and was beloved in both. A nuanced, technical striker who linked play brilliantly, Zamora was integral to Fulham's best Premier League seasons and was part of the squad that reached the 2010 Europa League Final. He was the kind of player Fulham fans appreciate: intelligent, skilful, and utterly committed to the cause.
achievements: Two separate spells at Craven Cottage
Part of the 2010 Europa League Final squad
Consistent contributor across multiple Premier League seasons
Embodied Fulham's measured, technical approach

legends.name: Brian McBride
legends.story: The American striker became one of the most beloved players in Fulham's history — a wholehearted, courageous centre-forward who led the line with bravery and heart. McBride's blood-on-his-forehead commitment to every match won him the adoration of the Cottage faithful. He proved that character can be as valuable as talent, and Fulham fans still sing his name.
achievements: USA international who became a Fulham icon
Known for fearless physicality and dedication
Key figure in mid-2000s Premier League survival seasons
Inducted into the National Soccer Hall of Fame

iconic_moments.title: 2010 Europa League Run — The Magical Night at the Cottage
iconic_moments.significance: Trailing 3-1 from the first leg, Fulham produced one of the greatest European nights in English football history. Bobby Zamora's late goal in the second leg sealed a stunning 4-1 aggregate victory over Juventus. Roy Hodgson's men went on to reach the Europa League Final in Hamburg — an achievement beyond any Fulham fan's wildest dreams.

iconic_moments.title: Mitrović's 43-Goal Championship Season
iconic_moments.significance: Aleksandar Mitrović shattered the Championship scoring record in 2022-23, netting 43 times as Fulham stormed to the title. It was the most dominant individual Championship season in history, and it sent Fulham back to the Premier League as champions with a swagger that few expected from a club of their size.

iconic_moments.title: Championship Play-Off Final Win 2018
iconic_moments.significance: Ryan Sessegnon's goal at Wembley sent Fulham back to the Premier League after four years in the Championship. The 20-year-old academy product scoring the winner at the national stadium was the perfect Fulham story — youth, tradition, and the beautiful game done right.

biggest_tragedies.title: Yo-Yo Club Reputation — 7 Promotions and Relegations
biggest_tragedies.significance: Fulham's identity has been defined as much by instability as by beauty. Seven promotions and relegations in the modern era have made them perpetual oscillators between the top flight and the Championship. For a club with Craven Cottage and genuine history, the yo-yo existence has been a source of both frustration and perverse pride.

biggest_tragedies.title: 2010 Europa League Final Defeat
biggest_tragedies.significance: After the miracle against Juventus and the fairy-tale run through Europe, Fulham fell agonisingly short in Hamburg. Diego Forlan's extra-time winner for Atletico Madrid broke Fulham hearts. They had the greatest night in club history taken away in extra time — still the hardest night many Fulham fans can remember.

biggest_tragedies.title: Leaving Craven Cottage — The Temporary Exile
biggest_tragedies.significance: Briefly forced to play matches away from Craven Cottage due to safety and redevelopment concerns, Fulham's temporary exile from their riverside home was deeply uncomfortable. For a club whose identity is so tied to that unique ground — the river, the Cottage itself, the Putney End — playing elsewhere felt like being homeless.

chelsea.name: West London Derby
banter: Chelsea have no soul, just money.
We were here before Roman and we'll be here after.
Stamford Bridge is a building site. Craven Cottage is a cathedral.
Chelsea fans couldn't find West London on a map without a chauffeur.

brentford.name: West London Rivalry
banter: Brentford? Cute little club, bless them.
They've discovered data. We discovered football first.
The Thames runs past Craven Cottage, not Gtech. That says everything.

coaches.name: Jean Tigana
achievements: Won the First Division title in 2000-01 with a record 101 points
Took Fulham from Division One to the Premier League
Established Craven Cottage as a genuine top-flight venue
Signed Louis Saha and other quality continental players

coaches.name: Roy Hodgson
achievements: Kept Fulham in the Premier League in his first season
Masterminded the miraculous 2010 Europa League Final run
Defeated Juventus 4-1 on aggregate — one of English football's great European nights
Transformed Fulham from relegation candidates to continental competitors

coaches.name: Marco Silva
achievements: Won the Championship title in 2021-22
Secured back-to-back top-flight campaigns
Mitrović's record 43-goal season under his management in 2022-23
Established Fulham as a stable Premier League club

stadium.name: Craven Cottage
stadium.atmosphere_note: One of English football's most charming and unique grounds. Set on the banks of the Thames in Putney, with the iconic Cottage building in the corner and river views from certain stands, Craven Cottage has an intimacy and character that modern stadia cannot replicate. The Riverside Stand offers genuinely one-of-a-kind matchday views.
